behavior_summary: FGRENADE::look: When a player/actor inspects the grenade/gem item, the script reads the item's frame and makes the inspecting actor 'bark' a short description. Frames map to three descriptions: low frames -> 'fire gem', mid frames -> 'chaos gem', high frames -> 'unstable gem'.

FGRENADE::use: Using/activating the grenade checks its quantity, decrements it, updates frame/animation state (and may spawn a short process for fuse/animation). It then attempts to spawn a throwable projectile (Item::legal_create) and, on success, applies hurl parameters (Item::hurl) with randomized offsets; on failure or in alternate paths it invokes igniteChaos at the map coordinates. The use path plays a sound, creates visual sprites/effects, and destroys the original item.
FGRENADE::hit and FGRENADE::gotHit: Present as event stubs that set process info/process-exclude and immediately return in the provided excerpt (no gameplay effect shown).
notable_control_flow: FGRENADE::look branches by a mapped frame value: frame < 4 => 'fire gem', 4 <= frame < 8 => 'chaos gem', otherwise => 'unstable gem'.
FGRENADE::use early-returns if Item::getQ reports quantity == 0 (guard).
FGRENADE::use decrements quantity and updates frame; if the (post-update) frame equals 0 it calls a specific METHOD::0676 path; otherwise it proceeds to spawn/hurl logic.

Projectile creation path: loop/compute randomized offsets -> Item::legal_create to spawn projectile -> on success call Item::hurl with randomized params; on failure or other branch call igniteChaos at item coordinates.
After spawn/hurl or igniteChaos the script plays a sound (playSFX), creates visual sprite(s) (createSprite), and destroys the original FGRENADE item (Item::destroy).
open_questions: Precise operations and parameter semantics of FREE::2A56 and FREE::1A96 (they appear to map/normalize frame values and compute randomized coordinate offsets respectively).
Exact parameter ordering and coordinate convention for igniteChaos (code suggests z,y,x ordering in calls; this is inferred).
Purpose and effects of METHOD::0676 spawned when frame==0 (inferred to trigger item-specific behavior but details unknown).
Full semantics of the spawned FREE::1C19 process (likely a fuse/timer/visual process) — timing and interactions are inferred from usage.
Precise numeric meanings of frames (which frame values correspond to which grenade subtype beyond the compared thresholds) and whether frame changes persist across uses.

main_function.name: U8P_FGRENADE::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an inspect/U8P_FGRENADE::look event on the FGRENADE item by determining its subtype from the item's frame and making the inspecting actor emit a short descriptor ('fire gem', 'chaos gem', or 'unstable gem').
entry_conditions: Event 0 — triggered when an actor looks at or inspects the FGRENADE item (invoking process provides pid implicitly).

function_map.name: U8P_FGRENADE::look
function_map.role: Examine/description handler for FGRENADE items; maps frame to short bark strings for the inspecting actor.

function_map.name: U8P_FGRENADE::use
function_map.role: Activation/usage handler: checks quantity, updates frame/animation, may spawn fuse/process, attempts to spawn and hurl a projectile or triggers an on-ground explosion (igniteChaos), plays SFX/creates sprites, and destroys the original item.

function_map.name: U8P_FGRENADE::hit
function_map.role: Hit event stub — sets process info/process-exclude and returns (no additional behavior in excerpt).

function_map.name: U8P_FGRENADE::gotHit
function_map.role: Got-hit event stub — sets process info/process-exclude and returns (no additional behavior in excerpt).

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's current frame/index used to decide description category.
Post-process or normalize the frame value before selecting the description string (exact mapping unclear).
Instruct the inspecting actor/process (pid) to emit a short text string describing the grenade subtype.
Read the current frame to drive use/animation logic and decide behavior branches.
Read the item's quantity/charges to guard whether use proceeds (early-return if zero).
Update the item's quantity after a use (decrement/set new quantity).
Update the item's frame/animation index to reflect state changes caused by use.
Spawn a short process for fuse/timer/visual animation while grenade is active or being thrown.
Call/spawn a method when frame==0; likely triggers specific behavior tied to that frame value (uncertain).
Compute adjusted coordinates or randomized offset values for projectile spawn/hurl parameters (used repeatedly with map coords).
Generate pseudo-random values used to vary hurl timing/velocity/offsets.
Attempt to create the thrown projectile sprite/item at computed coordinates (returns success/created id).
Apply throw/hurl parameters (velocity/direction) to the newly created projectile item when creation succeeds.
Trigger the grenade's explosion/chaos effect at a map location (used as fallback or ground-use path).
Play sound effect associated with grenade activation/throw/explosion.
Create visual sprite/effect at/near the item's position to show the throw or explosion animation.
Destroy/remove the original FGRENADE item instance after it has been used/spawned/thrown.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: FGRENADE::look: When a player/actor inspects the grenade/gem item, the script reads the item's frame and makes the inspecting actor 'bark' a short description. Frames map to three descriptions: low frames -> 'fire gem', mid frames -> 'chaos gem', high frames -> 'unstable gem'.
